name: CalEthos, Inc.
business: CalEthos, through its subsidiary TerraVolt, is a developer of large-scale infrastructure focused on a master-planned data center campus with onsite natural gas power generation. It provides construction-ready building sites that are pre-permitted and fully equipped with power, utilities, and fiber connectivity to hyperscalers, colocation providers, and data center developers.
sellsTo: large-scale technology and cloud service providers

exposures.sentence: Our business model is focused on the infrastructure development stage of data center construction, where we provide construction-ready sites that are pre-permitted and approved, and fully-equipped with the power and utilities necessary for a data center company to build and operate its data center facilities.
This growth is fundamentally driven by the scaling of Artificial Intelligence ( AI ), which is anticipated to represent half of all workloads by 2030.
We intend to offer a natural gas-powered energy platform that provides 24/7 baseload power.

Next-generation designs often combine natural gas with Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) and solar, positioning the data center as a dynamic asset that can support the grid while maintaining its own critical load.
Nuclear potential participation in the northwest U.S. nuclear corridor for future Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) upgrade for additional clean baseload power.

TerraVolt's proposed solution is a Physical Infrastructure-as-a-Service (PIaaS) platform that will integrate onsite behind-the-meter (BTM) power with construction-ready data center building sites that include utilities and fiber connectivity.

catalysts.event: Secure land-use and conditional zone change approvals
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate securing land-use and conditional zone change approvals by year-end 2026.

catalysts.event: Submit design and environmental documentation for onsite natural gas power plant
catalysts.sentence: Additionally, we expect to submit to applicable state agencies all design and environmental documentation for the onsite natural gas power plant by mid-2026.
We expect to file these reports before the end of 2026, with the aim of securing all necessary construction approvals by the second quarter of 2027.

tamClaims.claim: Total capital requirements projected to reach $3 trillion by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: According to PricewaterhouseCoopers ( PwC ), the data center industry has entered a profound infrastructure investment supercycle, with total capital requirements projected to reach $3 trillion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Global data center capacity expected to nearly double from 103 GW to 200 GW between 2026 and 2030
tamClaims.sentence: Global data center capacity is expected to nearly double, growing from 103 GW to 200 GW between 2026 and 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Global market size valued at $386.71 billion in 2025, projected to exceed $1.1 trillion by 2035
tamClaims.sentence: The global market size was valued by PwC at approximately $386.71 billion in 2025 and is projected to exceed $1.1 trillion by 2035.
